name: Chroma.Chroma

values.name: average
values.comment:
Find the average of a non-empty list of colors, first converting them to the same color space mode.

Only supports RGBA, CYMK and LAB.
values.type: Chroma.Types.Mode -> List.Nonempty.Nonempty Chroma.Types.ExtColor -> Result.Result String.String Chroma.Types.ExtColor

values.name: averageChroma
values.comment:
Find the average of a non-empty list of colors defined as strongs, first converting them to the same color space mode.

Only supports RGBA, CYMK and LAB.
values.type: Chroma.Types.Mode -> List.Nonempty.Nonempty String.String -> Result.Result String.String Chroma.Types.ExtColor

values.name: chroma
values.comment: Given a valid hex string (8, 6 or 3) produce an RGB Color.

values.name: classes
values.comment:
Return a new configuration and a function from a float to color using the default configuration, the given
colors and the total number of colors (bins) to return.

values.name: classesWithArray
values.comment:
Return a new configuration and a function from a float to color based on the given configuration values, the given
colors and a predefined set of breaks/classes.
values.type: List.Nonempty.Nonempty Basics.Float -> Chroma.Scale.Data -> ( Chroma.Scale.Data, Basics.Float -> Chroma.Types.ExtColor )

values.name: colors
values.comment: Return a configuration and a list of colors based on the number of equal distance buckets to create and a list of colors.
values.type: Basics.Int -> List.Nonempty.Nonempty Chroma.Types.ExtColor -> ( Chroma.Scale.Data, List.Nonempty.Nonempty Chroma.Types.ExtColor )

values.name: colorsF
values.comment:
Return a configuration and a list of colors based on the number of equal distance buckets to create and
and a function given a value from 0 -> 1 produces a new color.

values.name: colorsWith
values.comment: Return the data with the given colors and a new list of colors.

values.name: distance
values.comment: Calculate the distance for a given color space.

values.name: distance255
values.comment: Calculate the distance in RGB 255 color space.

values.name: domain
values.comment:
Return a new configuration and a function from a float to color based on a new domain and list colors.
If using a list of colors, they must be the same length as the domain or only the first and last values will be used.
values.type: List.Nonempty.Nonempty Basics.Float -> List.Nonempty.Nonempty Chroma.Types.ExtColor -> ( Chroma.Scale.Data, Basics.Float -> Chroma.Types.ExtColor )

values.name: domainF
values.comment:
Return a new configuration and a function from a float to color based on a new domain
and a function given a value from 0 -> 1 produces a new color.
values.type: List.Nonempty.Nonempty Basics.Float -> (Basics.Float -> Chroma.Types.ExtColor) -> ( Chroma.Scale.Data, Basics.Float -> Chroma.Types.ExtColor )

values.name: domainWith
values.comment:
Return a new configuration and a function from a float to color based on an existing configuration and a new domain.
If using a list of colors, they must be the same length as the domain or only the first and last values will be used.
values.type: Chroma.Scale.Data -> List.Nonempty.Nonempty Basics.Float -> ( Chroma.Scale.Data, Basics.Float -> Chroma.Types.ExtColor )

values.name: limits
values.comment:
Create breaks/classes based on the data given.

Supports: CkMean (a variant of kMeans), Equal, Logarithmic, and Quantile.
values.type: Chroma.Limits.Limits.LimitMode -> Basics.Int -> List.Nonempty.Nonempty Basics.Float -> List.Nonempty.Nonempty Basics.Float

values.name: mix
values.comment: Mix two colors, first converting them to the same color space mode and then with a ratio.

values.name: mixChroma
values.comment: Mix two colors defined as a string, first converting them to the same color space mode and then with a ratio.
values.type: Chroma.Types.Mode -> Basics.Float -> String.String -> String.String -> Result.Result String.String Chroma.Types.ExtColor

values.name: name
values.comment:
Given a color turn it into a W3CX11 name or fall back to RGB string.

Types.RGBAColor (Color.rgb255 255 0 255) |> Chroma.name

values.name: padding
values.comment: Remove a fraction of the color gradient (0 -> 1). Applies both sides the same.
values.type: Basics.Float -> List.Nonempty.Nonempty Chroma.Types.ExtColor -> ( Chroma.Scale.Data, Basics.Float -> Chroma.Types.ExtColor )

values.name: paddingBoth
values.comment: Remove a fraction of the color gradient (0 -> 1).
values.type: ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) -> List.Nonempty.Nonempty Chroma.Types.ExtColor -> ( Chroma.Scale.Data, Basics.Float -> Chroma.Types.ExtColor )

values.name: paddingBothWith
values.comment: Remove a fraction of the color gradient (0 -> 1).
values.type: Chroma.Scale.Data -> ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) -> ( Chroma.Scale.Data, Basics.Float -> Chroma.Types.ExtColor )

values.name: paddingWith
values.comment: Remove a fraction of the color gradient (0 -> 1). Applies both sides the same.

values.name: scale
values.comment: Return a configuration and a function from a float to color based on default values and a list of colors.

values.name: scaleDefault
values.comment: Return a configuration and a function from a float to color based on default values - colors White to Black, domain 0 - 1.

values.name: scaleF
values.comment:
Return a configuration and a function from a float to color based on default values
and a function given a value from 0 -> 1 produces a new color.

values.name: scaleWith
values.comment: Return a new configuration and a function from a float to color based on the given configuration values and the given colors.

name: Chroma.Colors.Brewer
Chroma.Colors.Cividis
comment:
![cividis](https://github.com/marcosci/cividis)

Enables nearly-identical visual-data interpretation to color vision deficiency (CVD) people. It is perceptually
uniform in hue and brightness, and increases in brightness linearly.

name: Chroma.Colors.Turbo
comment:
![Turbo](https://ai.googleblog.com/2019/08/turbo-improved-rainbow-colormap-for.html), An Improved Rainbow Colormap for Visualization

A colormap that has the desirable properties of Jet while also addressing some of its shortcomings, such as
false detail, banding and color blindness ambiguity.

name: Chroma.Colors.W3CX11

values.name: color
values.comment:
Lookup a color by value.

    Color.rgb 0 255 255
    --> Ok "aqua" : Result String String

values.name: named
values.comment:
Lookup a color by name.

    named "cyan"
    --> Ok (RgbaSpace 0 1 1 1) : Result String Color.Color

values.name: hslaDegrees2rgb
values.comment: Hue is measured in degrees and wrapped to the range 0..360. Saturation and Lightness are clamped 0..1.

name: Chroma.Interpolator

values.name: interpolate
values.comment:
Return a new color based on interpolating on two colors and a weighting between them.

CMYK may not work correctly. Will return black if the types are not the same.

name: Chroma.Limits.Analyze

aliases.name: Scale
aliases.type: { min : Basics.Float, max : Basics.Float, sum : Basics.Float, values : List.Nonempty.Nonempty Basics.Float, count : Basics.Int }

values.name: analyze
values.comment: Given data, create a Scale data structure (min, max, sum, sorted values and number of values).

name: Chroma.Limits.CkMeans
comment:
A log linear implementation of [CkMeans](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/Ckmeans.1d.dp/index.html).
It uses a divide-and-conquer algorithm to compute a row in the dynamic programming matrix in O(n log n) time. The
[Simple Statistic's implementation](https://simplestatistics.org/docs/#ckmeans) was used to validate this
implementation.

There is a better one (not implemented) using the [SMAWK](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SMAWK_algorithm) optimisation.

The limit function produces class breaks like the other implementations and binned puts all data values into their
bins.


# Definition

@docs binned, limit


# Helpers

@docs converge, defaultResult, fillRestOfMatrix, firstLine, getMatrixIndexes, getValues

values.name: binned
values.comment:
Return the values in Scale into the given number of bins.

    Analyze.analyze (Nonempty.Nonempty 3 [1,3,4,3])
    |> binned 3
    -->  Nonempty.Nonempty [1] [[3,3,3], [4]]

values.name: converge
values.type: Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Float -> Chroma.Limits.CkMeans.CkRest -> ( Basics.Float, Basics.Int )

values.name: limit
values.comment:
Create up to bins number of results using the given scale.

    Analyze.analyze (Nonempty.Nonempty 3 [1,3,4,3])
    |> limit 3
    -->  Nonempty.Nonempty 1 [3,4]

values.comment: Given a data change the lightness value (need to be LAB).

values.comment: The default color map - black to white.

values.comment: Sensible default configuration defaults: RGB, domain 0-1, pos 0, 1,and white and black color range.

name: Chroma.Types

aliases.name: CymkColor
aliases.type: { cyan : Basics.Float, magenta : Basics.Float, yellow : Basics.Float, black : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: HslaColor
aliases.comment: HSLA where the Hue, Saturation, Lightness and Alpha 0..1.
aliases.type: { hue : Basics.Float, saturation : Basics.Float, lightness : Basics.Float, alpha : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: HslaDegreesColor
aliases.comment: HSLA where the Hue is 0..360, ann Saturation, Lightness and Alpha 0..1.
aliases.type: { hueDegrees : Basics.Float, saturation : Basics.Float, lightness : Basics.Float, alpha : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: LabColor
aliases.type: { lightness : Basics.Float, labA : Basics.Float, labB : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: LchColor
aliases.comment: LCH where Luminance is 0..100, Chroma is 0..230, and Hue is 0..360.
aliases.type: { luminance : Basics.Float, chroma : Basics.Float, hue : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: Rgba255Color
aliases.type: { red : Basics.Int, green : Basics.Int, blue : Basics.Int, alpha : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: RgbaColor
aliases.type: { red : Basics.Float, green : Basics.Float, blue : Basics.Float, alpha : Basics.Float }
